name: Dr David Trilling
role: Chair, Department of Astronomy & Planetary Science
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary science • Solar system astronomy • Observational astrophysics
bio: Dr. David Trilling received his undergraduate degree in Earth and Planetary Sciences and Astronomy and Astrophysics from Harvard University in 1994. He spent a year at the Max Planck Institute for Aeronomy (now the Max Planck Institute for Solar System Research) in Katlenburg-Lindau, Germany, before beginning graduate school in 1995. He earned his PhD in Planetary Sciences from the University of Arizona in 1999. He was a postdoctoral scholar at the University of California, Santa Cruz/Lick Observatory from 1999 to 2000. In 2000 he moved to the University of Pennsylvania, where he served as a postdoctoral scholar (2000–2004) and Lecturer (2003–2004). From 2004 to 2008 he was Assistant Astronomer (research faculty) at the University of Arizona's Astronomy Department/Steward Observatory. Since 2008 he has been on the faculty of Northern Arizona University, in what is now the Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science.
